{{Quote|Man, she's a smooth piece of work. You could stack ice cubes on her head and they wouldn't melt. Unarmed, unless she was hiding a weapon somewhere ''I'' couldn't spot it. Very attractive, too, but there's something spooky about her.|Lando Calrissian, after a meeting with Guri|<ref name="SotE (novel)" />}}
'''Guri''' was a [[Human replica droid]], designed to be an almost perfect replica of a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]], and the only one of her kind engineered as an [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]]. She was created around [[7 BBY/Legends|7 BBY]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] researchers [[Simonelle]] and [[Massad Thrumble]] for [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]], head of the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] crime syndicate. After being sold to Xizor for nine million [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], Guri served as his lieutenant and was one of the few individuals the necessarily paranoid [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] believed he could trust. Xizor left Guri to attend to much of the day to day running of Black Sun and many incorrectly assumed that she was the leader of the syndicate. Guri also served as an assassin, eliminating several of Xizor's enemies, including [[Imperial Advisor/Legends|Imperial Advisor]] [[Fendrilon Koozar]], the [[Ubese/Legends|Ubese]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boushh/Legends|Boushh]] and the leaders of the rival company [[Ororo Transportation/Legends|Ororo Transportation]].

In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Xizor charged Guri with arranging the assassination of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], hero of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and son of Xizor's rival, the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. After an attempt on Skywalker's life by a Black Sun operative failed, Skywalker's sister [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] sought Xizor's help in uncovering who was behind it. Xizor sent Guri to rendezvous with Organa and bring her to him on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Skywalker later attempted to rescue Organa, infiltrating [[Xizor's Palace]] and confronting the [[Dark Prince]]. During the encounter, Guri fought with Skywalker, but was defeated by the young [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. However, Skywalker spared her life and Guri was able to escape the subsequent [[Destruction of Xizor's Palace|destruction of the palace]].

After the fall of Black Sun and the death of Xizor, Guri set out on a personal quest to locate Thrumble and get him to purge her assassin programming so she could start a new life, though Xizor's niece, [[Savan]], attempted to stop her and to ensure the [[Droid/Legends|droid's]] continuing service to Black Sun. Despite Savan's efforts, Guri was able to track down Thrumble on [[Hurd's Moon]] and was successfully reprogrammed. After defeating Savan, Guri teamed up with [[Dash Rendar/Legends|Dash Rendar]], working together as a mercenary team before agreeing to serve as the template for a new range of human replica droids.

==Biography==
===Creation===
{{Quote|One of only a handful of Human replica droids ever made… and the only one ever programmed to be an assassin. A perfect construct.|Massad Thrumble|<ref name="Evolution1">[[Shadows of the Empire: Evolution 1|''Shadows of the Empire: Evolution'' 1]]</ref>}}
[[File:GuriCreation-SotEE1.png|thumb|left|Guri, during her creation]]
By around [[7 BBY/Legends|7 BBY]],<ref name="7BBY" /> the [[Falleen/Legends|Falleen]] [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]] had secured his place as the leader of [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]], the most powerful crime syndicate in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="NEGC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> Xizor's position necessitated a certain degree of paranoia and, with little trust for those around him, the [[Dark Prince]] sought out a completely loyal lieutenant, one who did not suffer from the same fallibility as a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]].<ref name="SotE Sourcebook" />

Meanwhile, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] had begun research into the development of [[Human replica droid]]s, the project being led by [[Simonelle]] and [[Massad Thrumble]].<ref name="Fact File" /> The researchers were able to acquire both the plans and a prototype of a Human replica droid that had been developed as part of the aborted [[Project Decoy]], possibly with Black Sun's assistance, which would serve as the basis for Guri.<ref name="SotE Sourcebook" /> Though Guri was originally intended to be only a prototype,<ref name="TOTG">''[[Threats of the Galaxy]]''</ref> Xizor quickly saw the potential of the project and commissioned them to construct a Human replica droid to serve as his second in command.<ref name="SotE Sourcebook" /> The researchers modified Guri for Xizor's purposes and sold her to the Dark Prince.<ref name="TOTG" />

Guri cost Xizor nine million [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], and he considered her worth every [[Decicred/Legends|decicred]]. Her long, silky blond hair and clear blue eyes, combined with a fit and healthy looking figure, gave her the appearance of a beautiful [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|woman]] in her early twenties. Her voice was cool and even, with no discernible accent.<ref name="SotE (novel)" />

Guri's lungs breathed air and her heart pumped blood, although, like all her organs, they were entirely synthetic, consisting of [[bio-fiber]]s. Her bone structure was constructed of high-strength, polymer-covered alloys, and thousands of [[micro-gyro]]s embedded in her joints gave her a perfect sense of balance. Both her bones and organs appeared entirely Human when scanned, as did her flawless, [[Cloning/Legends|cloned]] skin.<ref name="SotE Sourcebook" /> The skin was the only truly organic part of Guri's body, cloned from genuine Human skin specifically for her.<ref name="TOTG" /> Her muscles, however, would not fool scanners—in order for them to provide her with greater strength, they were made of a material that scanned as an unknown tissue.<ref name="Rebellion Era Sourcebook">''[[Rebellion Era Sourcebook]]''</ref> One of the few ways that she could be seen to differ from Humans was that her body temperature was ten percent cooler than was considered normal, cold enough to kill a Human.<ref name="SotE (novel)" />

[[File:Guri_TSS.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Guri]]
Her [[AA-1 VerboBrain/Legends|AA-1 VerboBrain]] processor was extensively modified by Simonelle, the [[Ingoian]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist's]] biggest contribution to her development,<ref name="Fact File" /> to allow her to keep track of all the duties Xizor would require of her.<ref name="SotE Sourcebook" /> Positronic Human behavioral programs allowed her to act Human<ref name="Rebellion Era Sourcebook" />—she could eat, drink and perform any other function needed to pass for a normal woman. She was also capable of emulating all of the "more intimate" functions of a Human woman, and Xizor occasionally made use of her abilities in that regard.<ref name="SotE (novel)" /> The processor was also loaded with tactical assessment data, combat, espionage and [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] techniques.<ref name="Rebellion Era Sourcebook" /> Guri's processor was so sophisticated that even when Project Decoy was completed after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], it failed to produce a droid with Guri's level of autonomous thought.<ref name="EGD">''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref>

Guri's eyes hid advanced sensors, providing her with infrared, low-light and telescopic vision, while her locked-access memory recorded input from her sensors,<ref name="Attack on Endor">{{SWMiniCite|set=Attack on Endor Scenario Pack|link=swminiAOE.asp|archivedate=20190906170144}}</ref> including audio recording. She also had an integrated, encrypted [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] for secure communications.<ref name="TOTG" /> Guri's ability with languages was also impressive—she could read and write in both [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] and [[Falleen (language)/Legends|Falleen]], while her [[Vocabulator/Legends|vocabulator]] and translator unit allowed her to speak a variety of languages including Basic, Falleen, [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]], [[Rodese/Legends|Rodese]] and [[Twi'leki/Legends|Ryl]]. She was [[Ambidexterity|ambidextrous]] and could carry up to five [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]] in her internal storage.<ref name="Attack on Endor" />

Guri was unique as the only one of her kind programmed to be an assassin.<ref name="SotE (novel)" /> Upon completion, her programming was complemented by extensive exercise to tone her muscles and combat training to ensure that she had all the skills required,<ref name="Evolution1" /> including the use of a variety of weapons, though she would rarely need to use them.<ref name="Rebellion Era Sourcebook" /> With her training complete, Guri's speed, combat skills and stealth capabilities made her the ideal assassin.<ref name="SotE (novel)" />

===Service to Black Sun===
====A unique creation====
{{Quote|I shall remain the only one of my kind. To create others with my abilities would compromise my utility to my master. That cannot be allowed.|Guri, to Simonelle|<ref name="SotE Sourcebook" />}}
[[File:GuriStatuesque-SOTETC.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Guri becomes an assassin and enforcer for Black Sun.]]
After completing Guri, Simonelle and Thrumble went their separate ways—Thrumble continued to work for the Empire, while Simonelle started operating out of a hidden enclave in the [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]]. Soon after Guri was turned over to Xizor, he decided to ensure her uniqueness by preventing her creators from producing any more like her. Thrumble's position within the Empire guaranteed his safety, however the creator of her processor had no such protection.<ref name="Fact File" /> Therefore, Xizor ordered Guri to travel to Simonelle's workshop and kill the scientist. However, Simonelle had anticipated Xizor's actions and constructed a replica of himself. Arriving at Simonelle's base, Guri entered the workshop and approached what she believed to be her creator. Striking quickly, she knocked him back into a computer console. Wasting no time, Guri grasped his neck and squeezed until blood began to pour from his eyes, nose and mouth. The confrontation was over in seconds and Guri returned to her master. The real Simonelle went into hiding to prevent Xizor from learning the truth.<ref name="SotE Sourcebook" />

====Duties====
Guri became Xizor's most trusted employee, serving as his lieutenant, chief bodyguard and enforcer. The "Dark Prince" made use of Guri to perform much of the day to day running of Black Sun, her duties including gathering information from the organization's vast network of [[Spy/Legends|spies]], locating potential candidates to perform jobs for Black Sun, manipulating records to cover traces of their activities and organizing Xizor's meetings.<ref name="SotE (novel)" /> She also performed roles in his legitimate businesses, including appointing [[Tavvar Va'ran]] to the position of [[Master Chef]] at the [[Manarai]], an exclusive [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] restaurant which was part-owned by Xizor.<ref name="SotE Sourcebook" /> Xizor came to value her advice and would frequently ask her opinion of his plans. However, her duties extended beyond these administrative duties and Xizor frequently made use of her abilities as an assassin to deal with his enemies and to enforce loyalty within Black Sun.<ref name="SotE (novel)" /> Since she so frequently acted directly on Xizor's behalf, most of those outside Black Sun's upper levels assumed that Guri was in charge of the organization, an assumption which neither Xizor or Guri did anything to discourage as it provided him with a degree of freedom of movement and, from Guri's point of view, served as an added layer of protection for her master. Likewise, Guri's true nature was unknown to all but the Dark Prince.<ref name="Rebellion Era Sourcebook" />

====New friends====
{{Quote|I am Prince Xizor's primary bodyguard, Your Excellency. I assure you that I can protect you from Jiliac's guards.|Guri, to Durga Besadii Tai|<ref name="Rebel Dawn">''[[Rebel Dawn]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Guri-Galaxies.jpg|thumb|right|Guri, on Dathomir]]
After the death of [[Aruk Besadii Aora]]<ref name="Rebel Dawn" /> around [[4 BBY/Legends|4 BBY]],<ref name="4BBY">[[Aruk Besadii Aora]] is [[Death/Legends|killed]] towards the end of ''[[The Hutt Gambit]]'', which ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' places in [[4 BBY/Legends|4 BBY]].</ref> his son [[Durga Besadii Tai]] enlisted the help of Black Sun in his quest to become leader of the [[Besadii/Legends|Besadii]] [[Kajidic/Legends|kajidic]], having them eliminate his rivals.<ref name="The Hutt Gambit">''[[The Hutt Gambit]]''</ref> This left the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] in debt to Black Sun and Xizor intended to use Besadii to get a foothold in [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]] and to take a share of Besadii's [[Glitterstim/Legends|glitterstim]] operation on [[Ylesia/Legends|Ylesia]]. Durga, however, was unwilling to relinquish his power lightly. Guri suggested that she could eliminate Durga, but Xizor was adamant that it was better to manipulate the Hutt into falling into service to Black Sun.<ref name="Rebel Dawn" />

It was eventually Durga's desire to discover who was responsible for the assassination of his father that led him to commit to Black Sun.<ref name="Rebel Dawn" /> In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]],<ref name="1BBY">[[Jiliac Desilijic Tiron]]'s [[Death/Legends|death]] in ''[[Rebel Dawn]]'' occurs just after [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo's]] return from the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]] following the events of [[Star Wars: The Han Solo Adventures|''The Han Solo Adventures'']]. ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' places the end of ''The Hand Solo Adventures'' in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]].</ref> after an investigation uncovered evidence pointing to [[Jiliac Desilijic Tiron]], Xizor dispatched Guri to deliver the evidence in person to [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]] and to assist Durga in getting his revenge. Upon hearing the news, the Hutt was furious and set off to confront Jiliac under the [[Old Law]] with Guri at his side.<ref name="Rebel Dawn" />

After reaching Jiliac's [[Winter Palace]], the two Hutt leaders fought while Guri stood guard, ensuring that Jiliac's nephew, [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]], not get involved. When Jiliac lay dead, Durga emerged and, still angry but lacking the energy to fight Jabba, ordered Guri to kill him. She refused, stating that her orders were only to ensure Durga's revenge against Jiliac—Jabba was not to be harmed. Instead, she insisted that Durga leave with her.<ref name="Rebel Dawn" />

Sometime after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the Empire was conducting secret and dangerous scientific research on the remote planet [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]], intending to engineer the dreadful [[Imperial bioweapons Project I71A|Blackwing virus]] in order to create super [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]]. However, an accident happened in the [[Imperial Research and Prison Facility]] and resulted in the [[Blackwing virus outbreak on Dathomir|virus outbreak]]. Most of the Imperial staff was killed and became flesh-craving [[Zombie/Legends|undead]] creatures. Black Sun discovered the situation and Xizor decided to make use of the opportunity to obtain a sample of the virus. Guri, accompanied by two Black Sun enforcers, traveled to Dathomir aboard a [[YT-2400 light freighter/Legends|YT-2400 light freighter]] to investigate the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] recently established [[Quarantine Zone (Dathomir)|Quarantine Zone]]. While on Dathomir, Guri recruited [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] to conduct their own search of the Quarantine Zone, providing them with an encrypted code that would allow them to enter the zone, and telling them to make contact with [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Zahara Cody]] in [[Camp Alpha]].<ref name="Galaxies">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Mysterious Meeting Request|Dathomir}}</ref> After receiving an anti-virus from Doctor Cody,<ref name="SWG:GI">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Get Inoculated|Dathomir}}</ref> the spacers headed to the Imperial Research and Prison Facility in order to obtain a sample of the virus.<ref name="SWG:E">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Escape|Dathomir}}</ref>

====New enemies====
{{Quote|He tried to rascal Black Sun for more credits on a delivery for which there was an agreed-upon contract. That was… unwise.|Guri, about Boushh|<ref name="SotE (novel)" />}}
[[File:GuriWindow-SOTETC.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Guri, on a [[Space station/Legends|space station]].]]
When the [[Ubese/Legends|Ubese]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boushh/Legends|Boushh]] was hired by Black Sun but, after capturing the bounty, demanded fifty percent more than his contract had specified, Xizor sent Guri to meet with him. Guri took her ship, the ''[[Stinger]]'', to [[Gelgelar/Legends|Gelgelar]] for the rendezvous. Making her way to Boushh's location, she entered to find the bounty hunter standing behind a makeshift table, his weapon trained on her. Needing a distraction before she could strike, Guri went along with Boushh's demands, allowing him to frisk her for weapons and then placing a case full of credits on the table and opening it.<ref name="SotE Sourcebook" />

Boushh kept his weapon trained on Guri as he reached into the case to check it was all there. It was at this point that Guri got her distraction when the small [[Dinko (creature)|dinko]] hidden in the case bit Boushh's hand, the pain causing him to drop his weapon. Guri's high kick struck him on the head and snapped his neck. Crushing the dinko in her hands, Guri picked up Boushh's corpse and returned to the ''Stinger'', along with his [[Unidentified Nimbanel|Nimbanese prisoner]].<ref name="SotE Sourcebook" />

Another target of Guri's was [[Fendrilon Koozar]], an [[Imperial Advisor/Legends|advisor]] to [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] who was known to interfere with the business of others. During one of Koozar's visits to the Manarai, Guri approached Master Chef Va'ran and requested that he give Koozar a "house special", a code which indicated that he should discreetly poison Koozar's meal. She remained at the Manarai to watch over the chef's work, leaving only after the tampered food was served to Koozar. Following Guri's instructions, Va'ran used a combination of chemicals which triggered a hallucinatory episode in Koozar—after leaving the restaurant, the Imperial wrested the controls of his luxury [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] from his [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and was killed upon crashing into the [[Ministry of Land Management]].<ref name="SotE Sourcebook" />

When the small time [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Owal Sopil]], unaware that he was working on contract to Black Sun, attempted to skim cargo during runs, he was quickly apprehended and taken to a remote bunker complex on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon]] of [[Endor (planet)/Legends|Endor]] where the local Black Sun [[Vigo/Legends|Vigo]] would decide his fate. Guri was sent to accompany the Vigo on a visit to the bunker to deal with both Sopil and other matters personally. However, while she was there, Sopil's brother [[Reik Sopil|Reik]], aided by the smugglers [[Dash Rendar/Legends|Dash Rendar]] and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], mounted a rescue attempt. Guri joined the Vigo and three [[Abyssin/Legends|Abyssin]] guards in attempting to repel the rescue party.<ref name="Attack on Endor2">{{SWMiniCite|set=Attack on Endor Scenario Pack|pack=Mission 4: Smuggler's Rescue|link=swminiAOE.asp|archivedate=20190906170144|bypass=1}}</ref>

[[File:Guri_-_SWGTCG.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Guri confronts Rendar in Kor Vella.]]
At some point following the Battle of Yavin,<ref>''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' indicates that Dash Rendar began taking jobs for the Rebel Alliance after the events of ''[[Galaxy of Fear: The Doomsday Ship]]'', 11 months after the Battle of Yavin.</ref> Black Sun learned that the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] smuggler Dash Rendar was providing transportation to the [[Swoop racing/Legends|swoop racer]] [[Kimmi Chyler]] as she traveled between races, while secretly transporting cargo for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. Guri was dispatched to intercept the smuggler while he was on [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] for the race at [[Agrilat Region|Agrilat]]. Accompanied by a pair of Black Sun operatives, Guri tracked a pair of [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] spies to [[Kor Vella/Legends|Kor Vella]], where they met with Rendar and his spacer bodyguard to pass on a [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] containing Imperial battle plans. Soon after the Bothans departed, Guri confronted Rendar and his companion, but they fought back and were able to escape.<ref name="SWGTCG">{{SWGTCG|set=The Shadow Syndicate|scenario=Light Side scenario campaign}}</ref>

The final swoop race of the season took Rendar, Chyler and the bodyguard to [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. [[Xizor Transport Systems/Legends|Xizor Transport Systems]] was looking to sponsor a swoop racing team and Xizor held a party, to which the trio was invited. Guri was also in attendance with her master but refrained from confronting Rendar at the party. However, after the party, she and Xizor followed the group to a nearby [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] where Rendar was delivering a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] to the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and Rebel hero [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. Xizor ordered Guri to kill the group and to take the datapad, but Skywalker and the others put up a strong defense. As the fight spread into the Mos Espa streets, Xizor was knocked down and Guri moved to protect her master. Deciding that they had learned enough about Rendar's involvement, Xizor opted to retreat, with Guri covering him as they fled.<ref name="SWGTCG" />

On another occasion, Vigo [[Green]], in an attempt to discredit his rival, Vigo [[Sprax]], leaked a [[Barani List|list]] of Black Sun's operatives under Sprax in the [[Sisar Run/Legends|Sisar Run]] which tied several of Black Sun's cover operations back to the organization. Upon discovering the list's existence, Sprax mounted an operation to retrieve it before it could fall into the hands of the Imperial authorities. The datapad was eventually recovered by agents hired by [[Kalend Thora]] and [[Mal Biron]], on behalf of Sprax and Guri traveled to [[Yen-2]] to retrieve the datapad, which she promptly destroyed. More importantly, during the recovery operation, the agents had also uncovered information which implicated Green as the source of the leak. Returning to Coruscant, Guri informed her master of what had been discovered.<ref name="Secrets of the Sisar Run">''[[Secrets of the Sisar Run]]''</ref> Upon hearing that Green was a spy, Xizor called a meeting of all the Vigos where he had Guri throttle the traitor in front of the others. Green's violent death would serve as a warning to the other Vigos not to cross the Dark Prince.<ref name="SotE (novel)" />

====Ororo massacre====
{{Quote|Go home, little girl. Leave now while I still permit it. And don't come back. You do, and I might find a use for you you wouldn't like.|Tuyay's last words to Guri|<ref name="SotE (novel)" />}}
[[File:GuriTuyay-SOTETC.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Guri picks up Tuyay's chair.]]
Xizor later discovered that [[Ororo Transportation/Legends|Ororo Transportation]] had been attempting to take over Black Sun's [[Spice/Legends|spice]] operations in the [[Bajic sector/Legends|Bajic sector]] and he ordered Guri to send a clear message of the price of crossing him. Meeting with their representatives, [[Tuyay]], [[Dellis Yuls]] and [[Z. Limmer]], together with their [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]], Guri presented Black Sun's accusations which the group initially denied. Later in the meeting, however, Tuyay lost his composure and announced that they no longer intended to be used by Black Sun.<ref name="SotE (novel)" />

Moving quickly, Guri stood and leaped over the table picking up Tuyay and throwing him at the bodyguards, knocking them over before they had a chance to draw their blasters. Yuls moved to pull his own blaster but Guri grabbed his wrist and broke it before he could fire. Tossing the weapon away, she snapped his neck before piercing Limmer's throat with her fingertips.<ref name="SotE (novel)" />

As Tuyay got back to his feet, Guri grabbed his throat and began to choke him. Unaware that she was a droid, Tuyay futilely attempted to choke her back before losing consciousness. Taking a blaster from one of the downed bodyguards, Guri shot them both, together with Limmer and Yuls, in the head. Finally waiting for Tuyay to regain consciousness, Guri promised to pass on his message to Xizor, before shooting him in the eye. The entire confrontation was over in a matter of moments.<ref name="SotE (novel)" />

====Hunt for Luke Skywalker====
{{Quote|You are attracted to this woman in a romantic way.''"<br />"''So?''"<br />"''Such attractions have been known to cloud the minds of otherwise rational beings.''"<br />"''Do not fear, my dear Guri. She could never replace you in my affections.|Guri and Xizor discuss his feelings for Leia Organa|<ref name="SotE (novel)" />}}
[[File:Guri5.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Guri talks to Prince Xizor.]]
Shortly after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]<ref name="SotE (novel)" /> in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="SotE-date">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the events of ''[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'' in 3.5 ABY.</ref> Xizor learned that Luke Skywalker was the son of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Involved in a power struggle with the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], Xizor ordered the termination of Skywalker.<ref name="SotE (novel)" /> The Dark Prince trusted Guri to oversee the operation, instructing her to pursue several possible leads on Skywalker. She was to determine his location by retrieving information from within the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Rebel Alliance fleet]] and to attempt to bribe Alliance personnel to arrange for an accident to befall him. In addition, Xizor told her to have their operatives arrange for assassins in several key locations and to ensure that an operative followed [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], in case Skywalker attempted to rescue his friend [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], who had recently been captured by the bounty hunter.<ref name="SotE Sourcebook" />

Guri made use of the Vigos in carrying out her instructions to capture Skywalker. The [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Clezo]]'s "safari outfitting" service could be used to recruit a bounty hunter capable of tracking Fett without leaving any traceable connection to Black Sun, while the [[Nalroni/Legends|Nalroni]] Sprax had contacts within the Alliance, who frequently purchased starship parts from his salvage business. Guri started to get results within days, personally selecting a bounty hunter to pursue Fett and learning that he was heading for the [[Imperial Enclave|Imperial enclave]] on [[Gall/Legends|Gall]] in the [[Zhar system]]. When Sprax reported that Skywalker's former [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|group]], [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], was also headed to the Zhar system, to a hidden base on [[Kile]], Guri determined that there was a high probability of Skywalker making an attempt to rescue Solo in the [[Star system/Legends|system]].<ref name="SotE Sourcebook" />

Sprax also informed her that Rogue Squadron's chief technician, [[Viera Cheran]], was increasingly dissatisfied with her role within the Alliance. Hoping to enlist Cheran's help, Guri had one of Black Sun's moles approach the technician and offer her 50,000 credits to arrange a "technical malfunction" aimed at killing Skywalker if he were to rendezvous with the Rogues. Cheran agreed and Guri arranged for the credits to be transferred to her account, using a network of dummy financial corporations established by the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] Vigo [[Perit]] to ensure that the payment could not be traced to Black Sun.<ref name="SotE Sourcebook" />

[[File:GuriHoudini-SOTETC.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Guri breaks free of her restraints.]]
As Guri expected, Skywalker teamed up with Rogue Squadron in an attempt to rescue Solo on Gall. Cheran arranged for [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson's]] [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] to take control of his [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]] and fire upon Skywalker's during the mission. Skywalker escaped the initial assassination attempt and Cheran was killed by [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] while attempting to kill Skywalker upon his return to Kile. However, the situation turned in Xizor's favor when, unaware of the Dark Prince's involvement, [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] decided to make contact with Black Sun in the hopes of finding out who was responsible for the attempt on Skywalker's life. Xizor dispatched Guri to [[Rodia/Legends|Rodia]] to meet with the Rebel.<ref name="SotE (novel)" />

When Guri arrived at Rodia she was told to meet Organa at the [[Next Chance]] [[Casino/Legends|casino]]. Suspicious of Guri, Organa had arranged for some security in the form of Lando Calrissian and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] who would be nearby while she and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] met with the Black Sun representative. The initial meeting achieved little, with Guri claiming that she had business to attend to on a local [[Moon/Legends|moon]] and had to cut the meeting short, however it did leave the Rebels with the distinct impression that there was something unusual about Guri.<ref name="SotE (novel)" />

Returning to Coruscant, Guri was debriefed by Xizor. Guri felt that Organa looking into Black Sun was dangerous and advocated killing her, together with Calrissian, Chewbacca, C-3PO and anybody who knew they had been to Rodia to leave no traces. However, Xizor had other plans for Organa and told Guri to meet with her again and bring her to Coruscant so that he might personally find out what she knew.<ref name="SotE (novel)" />

When Guri returned to meet with Organa again, the Rebels had installed a scanner device which quickly confirmed their suspicions—Guri was not Human, although it could not identify what she was. With the scanner having failed, Organa had C-3PO put a sleeping potion in Guri's tea, hoping that during the couple of hours she was knocked out, the Rebels could conduct a closer examination of her person and effects. After that failed to have any effect the two continued to trade diplomatic small-talk until Guri tired of proceedings and attempted to abduct Organa only to be restrained by Calrissian, Chewbacca and Dash Rendar, who had arrived after the first meeting, while the group decided what to do. After much discussion, Organa agreed to go to Coruscant with Guri in the ''Stinger'', taking Chewbacca with her. To allow Organa to make it onto Coruscant without being identified, Guri provided her with Boushh's clothes and helmet, which she had kept after his death.<ref name="SotE (novel)" />

====Fall of Black Sun====
[[File:Xizor_escort_Edge_of_the_Empire.png|thumb|right|260px|Guri escorting Prince Xizor]]
Xizor attempted unsuccessfully to seduce Organa but she could still prove useful to him as bait for Luke Skywalker. Sure enough, Skywalker came to mount a rescue bringing Calrissian and Rendar with him and linking up with Chewbacca who Xizor had allowed to escape. However, Xizor had underestimated how capable the group was. After losing two units of guards, he ordered Guri to bring Organa to him.<ref name="SotE (novel)" />

Organa, however, was already prepared when the droid arrived. She hit Guri over the back of the head as she entered the room, a blow that would have rendered a Human unconscious, but which only caused Guri to momentarily lose her balance. It was enough for Organa to get outside and lock the door. As she ran, Guri began to smash the door down. After making her way out of the room, Guri returned to Xizor and the two of them set off to confront the intruders personally.<ref name="SotE (novel)" />

When they finally caught up with them a firefight broke out. While Skywalker deflected blaster bolts with his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], the unarmed Guri took a heavy chair and hurled it down the corridor towards them. Chewbacca shot it down with his [[Bowcaster/Legends|bowcaster]]. Realizing that they were outnumbered, Xizor retreated to a nearby room while Guri covered him and then called the guards for backup. As the guards arrived and a stalemate developed between the two groups, Guri slipped away.<ref name="SotE (novel)" />

====A worthy challenge====
{{Quote|I am what I am, Jedi. I don't think there can be any salvation for me.|Guri, to Luke Skywalker|<ref name="SotE (novel)" />}}
[[File:GuriFallDownGoBoom-SOTETC.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Guri fights Luke Skywalker.]]
The Rebels escaped after planting a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|bomb]], leaving only five minutes before [[Xizor's Palace]] was destroyed. As they fled, the group ran into Guri. Having seen his abilities in the previous fight, Guri had decided that Skywalker would be a worthy challenge for her abilities. She persuaded the young [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] to throw aside his lightsaber and fight unarmed. Using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], Skywalker was able to evade Guri's attacks. Momentarily losing his concentration, Skywalker was knocked to the floor by Guri who moved to strike a killing blow. With the help of the Force he was able to move away from her blow and used a leg sweep to send her to the floor, igniting his lightsaber.<ref name="SotE (novel)" />

Skywalker had defeated her, but rather than destroying her, he extended an offer for her to come with him and join the Rebel Alliance. Guri declined, explaining that overcoming the controls implanted in her programming would be impossible for the resource-strapped Rebels. Skywalker reluctantly abandoned her to the palace and made his way to the roof with the other Rebels to rendezvous with the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']].<ref name="SotE (novel)" /> Guri, however, made her own way to the roof and, taking a [[para-wing glider]], leaped from the top of the palace to safety.<ref name="Evolution1" /> As the Rebels took off, Skywalker noticed the missing paraglider and came to suspect that she had escaped.<ref name="SotE (novel)" /><ref name="Card-Topps-Xizor's Castle Blows Up">{{Topps|set=1996 Topps Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|cardname=Xizor's Castle Blows Up|link=https://www.rebelscum.com/photo.asp?image=/sote/soteTC065.jpg}}</ref>

===Life after Black Sun===
====Aftermath====
[[File:GuriWithGuns-SotEE.png|thumb|right|Guri, after the fall of Black Sun.]]
Xizor, too, escaped the [[Destruction of Xizor's Palace|destruction of his palace]] and attempted to stop the Rebels leaving Coruscant. However, the Dark Prince paid the price for his personal war with Darth Vader as the Dark Lord of the Sith arrived in his flagship, the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], and destroyed Xizor's [[Skyhook/Legends|skyhook]], the ''[[Falleen's Fist]]''.<ref name="SotE (novel)" /> The death of Xizor at the [[Battle over Coruscant]] left a power vacuum at the top of Black Sun, leading to a power struggle between the Vigos to take over the organization.<ref name="Evolution1" />

The death of her master also had a big effect on Guri. Dropping out of sight, Guri spent months coming to terms with what had happened and contemplating her future. She was aware that, with the knowledge and abilities she possessed, she could have taken over Black Sun herself and become rich and powerful,<ref name="Evolution2">[[Shadows of the Empire: Evolution 2|''Shadows of the Empire: Evolution'' 2]]</ref> yet at the same time she began to wonder just how Human she could be.<ref name="Evolution1" />

====Journey to Hurd's Moon====
{{Quote|Guri is the key to all of my uncle's secrets. I must have her to take over Black Sun.|Savan|<ref name="Evolution3">[[Shadows of the Empire: Evolution 3|''Shadows of the Empire: Evolution'' 3]]</ref>}}
Shortly after the death of Emperor Palpatine at the Battle of Endor in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="4ABY">''[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire: Evolution]]'' takes place shortly after the events of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].</ref> Guri began a quest to locate her designer, Massad Thrumble. Since retiring from Imperial service, Thrumble had kept out of galactic events and was now the owner of a cantina on [[Hurd's Moon]] which served as a front for his workshop.<ref name="Evolution1" />

The journey to Hurd's Moon would not be simple, however. While traveling in the ''Stinger'', Guri encountered a [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], still loyal to the Empire. The Imperials ordered Guri to shut down her engines but the droid had other intentions. With the Star Destroyer in pursuit, Guri activated a spread of [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] from her well-armed vessel, destroying the Imperial [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]].<ref name="Evolution1" />

While Guri conducted her search, she was unaware that there was another party looking for her.<ref name="Evolution1" /> On Coruscant, Xizor's niece and last surviving relative, [[Savan]], had [[Black Sun civil war|plans]] to take control of Black Sun.<ref name="Evolution2" /> Savan knew that Guri retained all of Xizor's secrets and that possession of the droid would give her a clear advantage over her rivals for the leadership of the criminal organization.<ref name="Evolution3" /> Shortly after Guri defeated the Star Destroyer, the Rodian [[Prevaro]] approached Savan with news of Guri's whereabouts. Savan, in turn, contacted the [[Hiitian/Legends|Hiitian]] bounty hunter, [[Kar Yang]], and hired him to locate and retrieve Guri.<ref name="Evolution1" />

====Rescue of Doc====
[[File:GuriLake-SotEE2.png|thumb|left|Guri emerges from the Kajiin Swamp on Murninkam.]]
After arriving at Hurd's Moon, Guri made her way to the cantina, after first fighting her way through a group of thugs who attempted to rob her. Once there, she finally made contact with Thrumble, who instantly recognized her, and what she was. Guri explained that she didn't want to be an assassin anymore and asked him to reprogram her, offering him whatever price he wanted. Thrumble agreed to help but refused the offer of credits, explaining that he was more interested in the challenge than in money. However, there was one thing preventing Thrumble from helping Guri—his associate, a unique [[Medical droid/Legends|medical droid]] called [[A-OIC|Doc]], had been kidnapped by [[Zan Pike|Zan]] and [[Zu Pike]] for delivery to the scientist [[Spinda Caveel]]. Without Doc to assist him, Thrumble would be unable to help Guri. In exchange for Thrumble's help, Guri set off for Caveel's base on [[Murninkam]].<ref name="Evolution1" />

As the ''Stinger'' approached Murninkam, Guri came under attack from droid-controlled picket starfighters. Though she succeeded in destroying one the second managed to hit her ship, causing her to crash into a [[Kajiin Swamp|swamp]] on the world below. Neither Guri or her ship was seriously damaged, however, and as the ''Stinger'' sank into the swamp, Guri surfaced and began to make her way through the forest.<ref name="Evolution2" />

The journey was not easy and Guri had to fight her way through the myriad predatory creatures that resided there. She eventually found her way to Caveel's base. Although it was protected by a [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shield]], Guri took advantage of a small hole, opened to allow the Pike sisters to pass through on their [[Swoop/Legends|swoops]], leaping through the hole after them to gain entrance to the facility.<ref name="Evolution2" />

Once inside, Guri made her way to the laboratory, where she confronted Caveel and the [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] sisters. As the Pike sisters reached for their blasters, Caveel stopped them. He too recognized what Guri was and wanted her taken alive. However, Guri easily foiled their attack, leaving them both unconscious on the floor. With some assistance from Doc, Caveel was incapacitated and the two droids fled the base on a swoop. Returning to the swamp, Guri summoned the ''Stinger'' which rose to the surface but she was unaware that Kar Yang had followed her to the planet and, as she prepared to leave, he attached a [[Homing beacon/Legends|homing beacon]] to her ship.<ref name="Evolution3" />

On Coruscant, Savan learned that Yang intended to betray her and keep Guri for himself. However, Savan had one advantage over the bounty hunter—Savan possessed Guri's control codes, based on her family's genetics, which would force the droid to obey her. Therefore, Savan headed for Hurd's Moon to take control of Guri.<ref name="Evolution3" />

====Reprogramming====
[[File:GuriPluggedIn-SotEE4.png|thumb|right|250px|Guri undergoes her reprogramming.]]
The ''Stinger'' arrived back at Hurd's Moon, pursued by Yang. Returning with Doc to Thrumble's cantina, Guri was taken to the scientist's laboratory to begin her reprogramming. The risks involved were great—one slight miscalculation could cause Guri's entire mind to be wiped—yet despite this, Guri was adamant that she wanted to proceed. Just as the procedure was about to begin, Yang arrived. With his weapon trained toward Guri, Yang ordered the HRD to secure Thrumble and then led her out. However, Yang had underestimated Guri's abilities. Waiting until they were clear of Thrumble, she pounced on Yang,<ref name="Evolution3" /> tearing at his heart with her bare hand.<ref name="Databank">{{DB|droid|guri|Guri}}</ref> Moments later, the bounty hunter lay dead on the floor.<ref name="Evolution3" />

After recalibrating his instruments,<ref name="Evolution3" /> Thrumble once more connected Guri and he and Doc began the reprogramming. When the procedure was complete Guri awoke with no memory of her time at Black Sun. There was no time to ascertain how successful the operation had been—Savan had arrived with a group of mercenaries to capture Guri. To make matters worse, Caveel and the Pike sisters had also come to Hurd's Moon to reclaim Doc, along with Guri's head, having abandoned hopes of taking her alive.<ref name="Evolution4">[[Shadows of the Empire: Evolution 4|''Shadows of the Empire: Evolution'' 4]]</ref>

As alarms rang out in Thrumble's laboratory, alerting him to the presence of the intruders, another group arrived—Leia Organa and Lando Calrissian had uncovered Savan's plot and, accompanied by Luke Skywalker, Han Solo and the droids [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and C-3PO, had followed her to Hurd's Moon. The interference of the Rebels was enough to give the Pike sisters cause to reconsider the arrangement. Deciding to leave, the two killed their employer in the process. Savan, however, would not be so easily put off.<ref name="Evolution5">[[Shadows of the Empire: Evolution 5|''Shadows of the Empire: Evolution'' 5]]</ref>

====Escape====
{{Quote|The number you called is no longer in service. Sorry.|Guri, after rejecting Savan|<ref name="Evolution5" />}}
[[File:GuriWhakk-SotEE5.png|thumb|left|220px|Guri defeats Savan.]]
With no time to explain to Guri why so many people were after her, Thrumble and the two droids fled the laboratory, hoping to use the ''Stinger'' to escape. Their progress was slowed when Guri began to suffer delayed synaptic shock from her reprogramming and Thrumble had to support her as she walked. This allowed Savan's thugs to catch up with them. Acting on instinct, Guri defeated the mercenaries easily. Although she had forgotten what she used to do, she could still remember how to do it.<ref name="Evolution5" />

The group resumed their course for the ''Stinger'' but Savan herself soon found them. With her weapon drawn, she offered Guri a job working for her. When Guri refused, Savan whispered Guri's control codes. At first it appeared that the droid was indeed under Savan's control, but once the Falleen dropped her guard, Guri turned on her, knocking her to the floor. Later, as Savan was led away by the authorities, Thrumble explained to Skywalker about Guri's reprogramming. After speaking to her and realizing that she truly had no memory of her former life, Skywalker decided to let her go, giving her the chance of a normal life.<ref name="Evolution5" />

====A new life====
{{Quote|There are parts based on hers in me, as well as all of our clients. She is our mother, if you like, and is held in great reverence.|[[Stanton Rendar]], on Guri|<ref name="Or Die Trying">{{InsiderCite|75|Or Die Trying}}</ref>}}
Afterwards, Guri walked into a bar, where Dash Rendar, also believed dead around the time of the death of Xizor, offered to buy her a drink.<ref name="Evolution5" /> The two eventually formed a mercenary team,<ref name="TDFS">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050407a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 1}}</ref> and were recruited by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] to help place a spy on the [[Tof/Legends|Tof]] fortress world of [[Saijo/Legends|Saijo]],<ref name="TFW">{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/nagai11202009/index.html|text=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs|int=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs}}</ref> teaming up with New Republic agents [[Kyle Katarn]] and [[Bey]] for the [[Battle of Saijo|mission]].<ref name="TDFS" /> Around this time, Guri was one of several [[Agent/Legends|agents]] who conducted shadow operations for the New Republic under the command of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]].<ref name="SPIN">{{SW|url=news/the-star-wars-spy-game-spin-declassified|text=The ''Star Wars'' Spy Game: SPIN Declassified|int=The Star Wars Spy Game: SPIN Declassified|nolive=1}}</ref> Knowing what Guri was, Rendar quickly conceived of a plan—by copying Guri's technology, he could create more of the valuable Human replica droids. Guri agreed to serve as a template for the development of more HRDs and served as Rendar's partner during the research and development phase. Afterwards, the two went their separate ways, though the development of the technology continued through [[Onadax Droid Technologies]].<ref name="Or Die Trying" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Don't play stupid, dirt flyer! My name is Guri. Black Sun wants some answers.|Guri, to Dash Rendar|<ref name="SWGTCG" />}}
[[File:GuriRipped-SotEE2.png|thumb|right|200px|Guri, after a fight]]
Although Guri physically resembled a Human female, there was a coolness about her which could be attributed to her true nature as a Human replica droid. This coolness was part of her ability to kill without any sign of conscience or emotion, and she often considered killing to be the easiest solution to problems.<ref name="SotE (novel)" />

Programmed to protect Xizor at all costs, Guri was devoted to him in all matters of his life—she was even known to hold his clothes while he spent time with a mistress. As Xizor's most trusted advisor Guri was in the unique position of being able to speak her mind, saying things that no other would dare say to the Dark Prince and he valued her counsel when it was offered. Even so, Guri was not one to speak unnecessarily and would generally not offer an opinion unless it was requested.<ref name="SotE (novel)" />

Although often terse, Guri exhibited a sometimes playful sense of arrogance; for example, when she massacred the leaders of Ororo Transportation, she took the time to allow Tuyay, knocked out in the initial fray, to regain consciousness so that she could have the last word before killing him. This smug attitude and apparent enjoyment of power were further illustrated by her dry and self-assured responses when Leia Organa and the other [[Heroes of Yavin/Legends|Heroes of Yavin]] interrogated her. At the end of this interrogation, when her captors moved to remove the metal bonds they had trapped her with, she burst them herself in a dramatic show of superhuman strength.<ref name="SotE (novel)" />

Guri also wanted to be stretched to her limits, preferring to perform jobs which she saw as challenging. It was this desire to push herself to her limits that led her to seek out a duel with Luke Skywalker.<ref name="SotE (novel)" />

Following the fall of Black Sun, Guri lost what had previously been her main purpose in life, to protect Xizor. It produced a dramatic change in her personality as she began to question her programming and to wonder if she could be something more.<ref name="Evolution1" /> Her quest for a new beginning was pursued with the same determination which she had always applied to her work with Black Sun, though neither her decision, or the process was easy for her and she was troubled by the memories of everything she had done.<ref name="Evolution2" />

Guri's reprogramming caused her to change further. Although she still possessed the ability to fight, she no longer had any memory of her time as an assassin, nor would she ever be one again with her assassination programming removed. She was also free to act independently, with her enforced loyalty to Xizor's family purged during the procedure.<ref name="Evolution5" /> She also showed signs of a sense of humor—initially pretending to have lost her entire memory during the reprogramming<ref name="Evolution4" /> and even smiling for the first time.<ref name="Databank" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Guri is Xizor's loyal, lethal, leggy henchperson.|Description of Guri from [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 9|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 9]]|<ref name="Galaxy9">{{GalaxyCite|9|Filling in the Shadows}}</ref>}}
Guri was created for [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|''Shadows of the Empire'']], a [[1996]] multimedia project set between [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], and appeared in its [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|novel]], [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (comic series)|comic]] and [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (video game)|video game]] incarnations.<ref name="SotE (novel)" /><ref name="SotE1">[[Shadows of the Empire 1|''Shadows of the Empire'' 1]]</ref><ref name="SotE-vg">[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (video game)|''Shadows of the Empire'' video game]]</ref> After [[Lucasfilm]] began working on the project in, author [[Steve Perry]] was brought in to write the novel for [[Bantam Spectra]]. At an early creative meeting at [[Skywalker Ranch]] in [[November]] [[1994]], Perry proposed several characters who were incorporated into the final story, including Guri, who Perry envisioned as Xizor's most able lieutenant, bodyguard and assassin. Early designs of the character were produced by [[Star Wars video games|game]] designer [[Jon Knoles]] and [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] artist [[Kilian Plunkett]].<ref name="Secrets">''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]''</ref> The [[Topps]] [[1996 Topps Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|''Shadows of the Empire'' trading card series]] featured paintings by [[Greg Hildebrandt|Greg]] and [[Tim Hildebrandt]] based on reference photographs; [[Olga Hildebrandt]] posed in costume as Guri for these images.<ref name="TAotBH">''[[Star Wars: The Art of the Brothers Hildebrandt]]''</ref> Guri appears in both the [[Wikipedia:Nintendo 64|Nintendo 64]] and [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] versions of the ''Shadows of the Empire'' video game and was voiced by [[Lisa Fuson]] in the PC version.<ref name="SotE-vg" /> At one point, [[Hasbro]]/[[Kenner Products|Kenner]] had plans to create a Guri action figure in their [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (toy line)|''Shadows of the Empire'' toy line]], but it was never carried forward. Concept art exists that shows the Guri figure would have come with a large gun and depicted her with "battle damage".<ref name="Secrets" />

[[File:GuriToyConcept.jpg|thumb|left|240px|Concept art of Kenner's planned Guri action figure.]]
Guri was later depicted in the ''"[[Reflections II: Expanding the Galaxy]]"'' set of [[Decipher]]'s [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]], in which she is portrayed by an unnamed model.<ref name="CCG">{{CCG|set=Reflections II: Expanding the Galaxy|cardname=Guri|archivedate=20210414211406}}</ref> She also appeared in Perry's [[1998]] comic series [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire: Evolution|''Shadows of the Empire: Evolution'']], illustrated by [[Ron Randall]], the [[2009]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies Trading Card Game]]'' expansion ''[[The Shadow Syndicate]]'' and the [[2003]] video game ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]''.<ref name="Evolution1" /><ref name="Galaxies" /><ref name="SWGTCG-Guri">{{SWGTCG|set=The Shadow Syndicate|cardname=Guri}}</ref> [[Joe Corroney]]'s [[:File:Xizor Guri BoShek.jpg|illustration]] of Guri in the [[Star Wars Insider 89|''Star Wars Insider'' 89]] article ''"[[Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy]]"'' was based on the appearance of artist [[Leah Mangue]].<ref name="Insider 89 Online">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/89/indexp2.html|text=Over My Dead Body -- Solo's Cover Story}}</ref>

Jon Knoles experimented with different colors for Guri's attire,<ref name="Secrets" /> before settling on the mix of red and gray which is seen in both the game and comic.<ref name="SotE1" /><ref name="SotE-vg" /> Guri's costume is similar in overall design in the Topps trading card series, but is colored yellow and blue and leaves her legs exposed.<ref name="SotE-card">{{Topps|set=1996 Topps Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|cardname=Guri|link=https://www.rebelscum.com/photo.asp?image=/sote/soteTC079.jpg}}</ref> The ''[[1997 Topps Star Wars Vehicles]]'' trading cards, ''Star Wars Galaxies Trading Card Game'' and ''"Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy"'' use the attire shows in the original trading cards,<ref name="SWGTCG-Guri" /><ref name="Vehicles">{{Topps|set=1997 Topps Star Wars Vehicles|cardname=''Stinger''|link=https://www.tcdb.com/ViewCard.cfm/sid/73005/cid/5259784/1997-Topps-Star-Wars-Vehicles-29-Stinger}}</ref><ref name="Insider89">{{InsiderCite|89|Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy}}</ref> while the ''Customizable Card Game'' image used a costume design following the comic series.<ref name="CCG" />

There is some uncertainty regarding the date of Guri's creation. The ''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]'' established that she was created by Simonelle based on the results of Project Decoy.<ref name="SotE Sourcebook" /> Massad Thrumble's involvement, together with that of the Empire, was not mentioned until ''Shadows of the Empire: Evolution'' and was clarified in later sources.<ref name="Fact File" /><ref name="NEGD" /><ref name="EGD" /><ref name="Evolution1" /> The novelization of ''Shadows of the Empire'' identified her skin as ten years old in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], suggesting that her creation was circa 7 BBY, and this has been repeated in several other sources.<ref name="SotE Sourcebook" /><ref name="SotE (novel)" /><ref name="SotE-date" /> Her inclusion in ''[[Rebel Dawn]]'' showed that she was indeed created prior to [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]].<ref name="Rebel Dawn" /><ref name="RD-date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' establishes that the events of ''[[Rebel Dawn]]'' start in 2 BBY.</ref> Yet Project Decoy was supposedly conducted by the Rebel Alliance,<ref name="SotE Sourcebook" /> which was still being established at this time.<ref name="Rebel Dawn" />
